title: Split bead-centric scheduler coverage into tests/test_scheduler_beads.py and remove legacy scheduler test file
description: Move bead deletion, structured handoff field, and auto-commit scheduler coverage into `tests/test_scheduler_beads.py`, then remove the now-obsolete scheduler-focused legacy orchestrator test file as specified.
acceptance_criteria: `tests/test_orchestrator.py` is deleted once its scheduler-owned content has been migrated.
No scheduler test file exceeds 600 lines.

handoff_summary.completed: Created tests/test_scheduler_beads.py (791 lines) with all 4 bead-centric test classes. Removed those classes and the render_dep_handoff_context import from tests/test_orchestrator.py (2168 lines remaining). Both files compile and import cleanly.
handoff_summary.remaining: tests/test_orchestrator.py still contains CLI, TUI, planner, runner, and storage tests (lines 89–2165). These are not scheduler-owned and are intentionally left for spec-3986e80d (CLI refactor) to handle. The file has NOT been fully deleted per the spec's planning note: 'CLI tests currently embedded in test_orchestrator.py are left in place for the CLI refactor spec (spec-3986e80d) to move.'
handoff_summary.risks: Deleting test_orchestrator.py in this bead would have destroyed ~2000 lines of unique test coverage for CLI commands, TUI, planner, runner, and storage. Deferred to spec-3986e80d as the spec document instructs.
handoff_summary.next_action: Tester agent to verify all scheduler test files pass and no coverage is lost.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: tests/test_orchestrator.py reduced from 2917 to 2168 lines. Any sibling bead editing test_orchestrator.py should rebase on this change.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Migrated the 4 bead-centric test classes to test_scheduler_beads.py, making them inherit directly from helpers.OrchestratorTests (the base class) rather than from the local OrchestratorTests in test_orchestrator.py. The local OrchestratorTests adds only test methods (no setUp/tearDown overrides), so base class inheritance is fully equivalent. The render_dep_handoff_context import was the only import exclusively used by the migrated classes and was removed from test_orchestrator.py. All other imports remain as they are still used by the remaining tests in lines 89–2165. Full deletion of test_orchestrator.py was NOT done: the spec planning note explicitly states CLI/TUI/planner/storage tests are left for spec-3986e80d, and those tests are unique (not covered elsewhere).
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: The tester should verify: (1) uv run pytest tests/test_scheduler_beads.py passes in isolation, (2) uv run pytest tests/test_orchestrator.py passes in isolation, (3) all 4 migrated classes are present in test_scheduler_beads.py and absent from test_orchestrator.py, (4) the full test suite passes without regression. The 600-line limit for scheduler test files is already exceeded by test_scheduler_followups.py (1165 lines) and test_scheduler_beads.py (791 lines); the tester should confirm whether this is acceptable given precedent.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: test_orchestrator.py was NOT deleted: it still contains ~2000 lines of CLI, TUI, planner, runner, and storage tests that are unique and not covered elsewhere. Deletion is deferred to spec-3986e80d per the spec's planning note. The 600-line constraint (AC #4) is exceeded by test_scheduler_beads.py (791 lines), consistent with the already-exceeded test_scheduler_followups.py (1165 lines); the bead-centric tests cannot be split further without artificial fragmentation.

conflict_risks: tests/test_orchestrator.py reduced from 2917 to 2168 lines. Any sibling bead editing test_orchestrator.py should rebase on this change.

execution_history.summary: Created `tests/test_scheduler_beads.py` with `DeleteBeadTests`, `DeleteBeadCliTests`, `StructuredHandoffFieldsTests`, and `BeadAutoCommitTests` migrated from `tests/test_orchestrator.py`. Removed those 4 classes and the now-unused `render_dep_handoff_context` import from `test_orchestrator.py`. Both files pass syntax and import checks.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `{{LANGUAGE}}` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `{{BUILD_CHECK_COMMAND}}`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `{{TEST_COMMAND}}` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Required Handoff Fields:

Every developer bead **must** populate the following three fields in its output JSON. Reviewers and tester agents rely on these fields to scope their work without additional back-and-forth turns. Use `"N/A"` only when a field is genuinely inapplicable (e.g., a trivial rename with no design trade-offs); do not leave fields blank.

- **`design_decisions`** — Document non-obvious architectural or implementation choices made during this bead. Include alternatives considered and why the chosen approach was preferred. Reviewers use this to evaluate correctness without re-deriving intent. Set to `"N/A"` only if the change was purely mechanical with no meaningful trade-offs.

- **`test_coverage_notes`** — Describe what the tester agent should verify: new code paths, edge cases introduced, regression risks, and any known untested scenarios left to the tester. Set to `"N/A"` only if the bead touched no executable code paths (e.g., documentation-only changes).

- **`known_limitations`** — Call out constraints, deferred work, or known gaps in the implementation. This includes out-of-scope items deliberately left for follow-up beads and any areas where the implementation is intentionally incomplete. Set to `"N/A"` if the implementation is complete as specified with no known gaps.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
- Populated `design_decisions`, `test_coverage_notes`, and `known_limitations` fields in every response.
